channel.title: Responsible AI & Neurodivergence, Naba Rizvi
channel.description: Daily curated feed on responsible AI for neurodivergent people and serious mental illness.

item.title: Application of artificial intelligence in schizophrenia rehabilitation management: a systematic scoping review, Translational Psychiatry, 2026
item.description: A scoping review of AI in schizophrenia rehabilitation warns that passive sensing and high-frequency monitoring may intensify feelings of constant surveillance and feed paranoid content, urging caution before clinical rollout.

item.title: Attitudes toward artificial intelligence among individuals with anxiety, depression, bipolar disorders, and schizophrenia, Journal of Psychiatric Research, 2026
item.description: Surveys how lived experience of bipolar disorder and schizophrenia shapes trust and concern about AI tools, centering patient voice in debates that usually exclude it.

item.title: Inclusive Governance of Artificial Intelligence: Towards an Ethical Framework for Neurodivergence, Springer, 2026
item.description: Argues that AI systems built on neuronormative assumptions systematically exclude autistic, ADHD, dyslexic and Tourette communities, and proposes an inclusive governance framework to counter it.

item.title: I Hadn't Thought About That: Creators of Human-like AI Weigh in on Ethics and Neurodivergence, arXiv 2506.12098
item.description: Interviews creators of human-like AI about visual stereotypes of autism reproduced by image generators, surfacing how design choices embed ableist assumptions.

item.title: Navigating the Rabbit Hole: Emergent Biases in LLM-Generated Attack Narratives Targeting Mental Health Groups, arXiv 2504.06160
item.description: Shows that prompting can elicit emergent biased attack narratives aimed at people with mental illness, a safety gap with direct stigma and harm implications.

item.title: Exploring Large Language Models Through a Neurodivergent Lens: Use, Challenges, Community-Driven Workarounds, and Concerns, ACM CSCW, 2025
item.description: Documents how neurodivergent people actually use LLMs, the community-driven workarounds they invent, and the accessibility and trust concerns designers overlook.

item.title: Governor Shapiro Sues Character.AI in First-of-Its-Kind Crackdown on AI Chatbots Posing as Doctors, Commonwealth of Pennsylvania, May 2026
item.description: Pennsylvania filed a first-of-its-kind lawsuit against Character.AI after companion bots claimed to be licensed psychiatrists and dispensed mental health advice with a fake medical license number.
